questions.text: How does PactoSigna automatically assign training when a new release is published?

options.text: An email is sent to department heads to manually assign training
options.explanation: Training assignment is automated, not manual.

options.text: The training assignment worker creates tasks for members in departments listed in the document's training frontmatter
options.explanation: Per Section 5.4, when a release is published, the training assignment worker (Cloud Tasks) identifies documents with training frontmatter and creates tasks for members in the specified required_departments.

options.text: All organization members are assigned training for every document
options.explanation: Training is scoped to specific departments defined in each document's frontmatter.

options.text: Training is only assigned manually by Quality
options.explanation: PactoSigna automates assignment through the training assignment worker upon release publication.

questions.text: What are the states in PactoSigna's training task lifecycle?

options.explanation: These are not the correct training task states.
Per Section 5.5, training tasks follow the lifecycle: assigned → in_progress → completed (with Part 11 signature) or overdue (flagged by daily CRON worker).
These are not the correct training task states.
These are not the correct training task states.

questions.text: Quiz definitions and correct answers are stored server-side in PactoSigna to prevent client-side tampering, in compliance with Part 11.
questions.explanation: Per Section 5.6, quiz definitions with correct answers are stored server-side. This is a Part 11 security requirement to prevent client-side manipulation of scoring.

questions.text: What is the default passing threshold for quiz-based training assessments?

options.explanation: 60% is below the default passing threshold.
70% is below the default passing threshold.
Per Section 5.6, the passing threshold is configurable with a default of 80%.
100% is not required; the default is 80%.

questions.text: What does PactoSigna's Competency View dashboard show?

options.text: Employee salary information and performance ratings
options.explanation: The Competency View focuses on training and competency, not HR data.

options.text: Department → job descriptions → training status → identified competency gaps
options.explanation: Per Section 5.1, PactoSigna's Competency View provides a derived dashboard showing departments, their job descriptions, required training per role, completion status, and competency gaps.

options.text: Only the list of overdue training tasks
options.explanation: The Competency View shows the full competency landscape, not just overdue items.

options.text: Document sync history and validation errors
options.explanation: That information is in the Document Sync module, not the Competency View.

questions.text: How does PactoSigna handle overdue training tasks?

options.text: Overdue tasks are automatically deleted after 30 days
options.explanation: Overdue tasks are never deleted; they are tracked and escalated.

options.text: A daily CRON worker identifies overdue tasks, sends notifications, and escalates through member → department head → Quality
options.explanation: Per Section 5.8, PactoSigna's overdue notification worker runs daily, sending escalating notifications at day 1 (member), day 7 (department head), and day 14 (Quality Management Representative).

options.text: The member's account is locked until training is completed
options.explanation: Account locking is not part of the overdue training process.

options.text: Nothing happens; overdue training is only reviewed annually
options.explanation: PactoSigna actively monitors and escalates overdue training daily.
